calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Deadline For Plaintiff To Amend Complaint And Request For Sanctions
Off Calendar Apr-21-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances (by Zoom):
Stephen Davis, Esq. 916-397-5978 for Plaintiff.
Thomas Koegel, Esq. 415-986-2800 Crowell & Moring LLP for Defendant.
Court reporter: Sheila Pham, CSR #13293, 415-274-9977, spham28@yahoo.com

ARGUED.
The Court states preparatory remarks and raises several issues.
Mr. Koegel presents arguments; Other counsel has not done what he has promised to do, prejudiced by this conduct, requests the Court to compel counsel with a date certain.
Mr. Davis disagrees on characterization of promise, motion to continue initiated by Defense' counsel, disagrees on prejudice. The Court denies sanctions. Parties agree the amended complaint will be filed by 5/12/16.

The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

Plaintiff's motion to set a deadline for plaintiff to file a second amended complaint and for sanctions. Parties agree to the filing of the amended complaint by May 12, 2026 hopefully without a motion. The Court denies sanctions. Parties to submit stipulation and proposed order to the Court in the next couple of days.

Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court reporter: Sheila Pham, CSR #13293, 415-274-9977, spham28@yahoo.com, Reported. (302/REQ)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT / Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Judgment, Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Nov-21-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances (by Zoom):
Stephen Davis, Esq. 916-397-5978 CALTRANS legal Division for Plaintiff.
Thomas Koegel, Esq. 415-986-2800 for Defendant.

Parties state appearance. The Court announces the tentative ruling. The Court states a timely notice of contest the TR was received from Defense.

ARGUED. Mr. Koegel presents arguments; state had no right under the statutes for Pacificbell to pay for the underground expense; what is the basis of the state claim, street and highway code section 673 on TR doesn't apply to Pacific Bell. The court comments and poses questions; needs to decide on initial burden, show affirmative evidence there is no triable issue; accrual issue. Mr. Koegel responds; Issue of "who pays". Mr. Davis presents argument, agrees with the TR. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT PACIFIC BELL TELEPHONE COMPANY, DBA AT&T CALIFORNIA MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT / Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication.

Pacific Bell Telephone Company, DBA AT&T California's Motion for Summary Judgment.

Taken Under Submission.***

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

***After the hearing, the court signs an Order Denying Summary Judgment.***
